name: Account-Based Selling
summary: A selling approach that treats a target account as a coordinated pursuit across stakeholders, functions, and moments rather than a single-threaded rep-to-buyer interaction.
whenToUseIt: Use account-based selling for strategic accounts, enterprise pursuits, and any deal where several people, teams, or channels must work together to win the account cleanly.
steps: Choose the account intentionally based on fit, value, and strategic relevance.
Coordinate stakeholders, messaging, and actions across the account rather than treating each touch as isolated.
Align internal resources and account strategy so pursuit feels deliberate and cumulative.
example: Instead of one AE running isolated outreach, account-based selling brings together SDR research, executive sponsorship, partner access, and role-specific messaging across a named enterprise account.
